1.name: Chupa Chups (Spain)
1.text: The logo of the brand was created by Salvador Dalí (Spanish painter) in 1969

2.name: Fish and Chips (United Kingdom)
2.text: Common takeaway food in numerous countries. The British governament safeguarded supplies of the dish's ingredients during the First World War.

3.name: Tatin pie (France)
3.text: Apple pie created in 1880s at Hotel Tatin by two sisters Stéphanie and Caroline Tatin

4.name: Tempura (Japan)
4.text: Japanese adaptation of the portuguese dish “peixinhos da horta”

5.name: Mandioca (Africa) (cassava in English)
5.text: It is used in pasta dishes especially in African cultures.

6.name: Quesadilla (Mexico)
6.text: Originaly from colonial Mexico (16th century) as a fusion between corn tortillas and dairy products like cheese.

1.name: Eiffel Tower
1.text: Iron lattice tower built in Paris in 1889

2.name: Colosseum
2.text: Ancient Roman amphitheatre in the heart of Rome

3.name: Taj Mahal
3.text: White marble mausoleum in Agra, India

4.name: Great Wall
4.text: Ancient fortification stretching across northern China

5.name: Machu Picchu
5.text: Incan citadel set high in the Andes Mountains

6.name: Statue of Liberty
6.text: Copper statue gifted by France to New York
